_comment: OPT-IN fragment. Paste into YOUR .claude/settings.json (hooks-only file). PATHS (0.3.9, field report cc_chobotnice 2026-07-16): ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} resolves ONLY inside hooks the plugin itself ships (hooks/hooks.json) — for hooks pasted into YOUR settings (this fragment) it is EMPTY, so the FALLBACK segment is what actually runs. Fill the fallback in ALWAYS: the default ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/pluginy/farky-memory-container suits an in-repo fork; otherwise point it at your plugin source checkout. NEVER point it at a versioned marketplace cache path (~/.claude/plugins/cache/<mkt>/<plugin>/<version>/) — the version segment changes on every upgrade and the hooks go silently dead. Memory paths stay project-relative — replace /memory with YOUR memory-dir consistently on ALL lines (a mismatched memory-dir on even one line splits the close-loop state from the injectors). Layers: (A) advisory echoes — harmless; (B) self-improvement close-loop (SessionStart init + boot-recovery / SessionEnd finalize / PreCompact guard) — invasive, writes memory-state under memory-dir; boot-recovery surfaces any UNCLOSED prior-session marker as a must-do-first advisory (run /close --auto <SID>), read-only, silent when none; (C) boot nerves (info-injection): state_inject/capability_inject/index_inject at SessionStart inject orientation + capability drift + folder-index map as ready facts, and journal_prompt at UserPromptSubmit silently feeds each prompt into memory/session.md (write-only); (D) drift warnings — adopter-drift (0.1.18) warns when your ACTIVE plugin version lags the newest in the marketplace cache (cache installs only) and fork-drift warns when an in-repo fork has fallen behind its source (reads memory-dir/.fmc-source; no-op for cache installs). None of this is force-wired into hooks/hooks.json — it crosses the trust boundary (invasive). If your settings.json uses bypassPermissions/broad allow-list, the self-mod classifier blocks editing it — put hooks in settings.local.json (hooks-only) instead.

hooks.command: echo 'FMC active (farky-memory-container). Silent work is good; silent fallback is forbidden. Read memory/MEMORY.md if present.'
echo 'FMC PreCompact: flush the live thread to memory/session.md and key orientation to memory/STATE.md before the window rolls — memory must not hang on the context window.'
